content.text:
```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Utility Sector Outflow on Mild Weather Pivot",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Open-Meteo daily heating degree days (HDD) and cooling degree days (CDD) for US population-weighted centers",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When rolling 7-day average HDD drops sharply from seasonal baseline, heating demand collapses, reducing utility stock appeal and triggering rotation out of XLU into risk assets.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Lower heating demand directly reduces utility revenue and crushes relative valuation of defensive utility stocks."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 7-day rolling HDD falls below historical seasonal average by >15% and XLU closes down 1%+ that day",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when HDD returns to seasonal norm",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Weather patterns shift weekly; mild snaps occur multiple times per winter/spring season, reliably triggering HDD collapses."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Freight Rail Utilization Recovery Signal",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "AAR (Association of American Railroads) weekly carload data via FRED series RAILCDF (carloads and intermodal) or scrape public AAR reports",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Weekly carload volume spikes above 12-week moving average signal sudden demand recovery in manufacturing and commodities, bullish for industrials and materials.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Rail volume is a leading proxy for real economic activity; spikes correlate with factory orders and industrial production acceleration."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly carload count exceeds 12-week MA by >5% and volume increases YoY",
      "exit": "After 3 weeks or when carloads fall back below 12-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal demand swings and supply-chain normalization events trigger rail spikes several times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Natural Gas Volatility Spike Reversal in Energy",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series DHHNGSP (Henry Hub natural gas spot price) daily closes for volatility calc",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When 20-day rolling volatility of natural gas prices spikes above 2-year 95th percentile, mean reversion in energy equities often follows as supply shocks resolve.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Extreme gas price volatility signals supply disruption; reversal attracts energy sector value buyers and reduces hedge costs."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 20-day rolling vol of DHHNGSP > 95th percentile over 2 years and XLE closes up >1% following a down day",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or when 20-day vol falls below 80th percentile",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal geopolitical and weather shocks drive gas price spikes multiple times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Network Expansion Sentiment Surge",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap API weekly count of newly added chargers across US; pair with Google Trends weekly search volume for 'EV charging stations near me'",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in charger installation and concurrent surge in consumer searches signal bullish infrastructure narrative, lifting XLK (chip/software) and XLI (automotive) positioning.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "EV charging expansion is a positive signal for semiconductor and software demand in automotive electrification."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly new charger count rises >20% QoQ and Google Trends 'EV charging' searches rise >30% YoY",
      "exit": "After 4 weeks or if charger growth rate decelerates below 10% QoQ",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 18,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Government subsidy cycles and corporate announcements cluster charger expansion; signal fires 3–4 times per year."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Mortgage Rate Compression Refinance Wave Fade",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series MORTGAGE30US (30-year mortgage rate) daily; pair with Google Trends weekly searches for 'mortgage refinance' and 'home equity loan'",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When MORTGAGE30US drops >0.5% over 2 weeks and refinance search interest spikes, consumer discretionary spending rebounds but eventual rate stabilization causes mean reversion.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Falling rates unlock household liquidity and home equity; consumers spend on discretionary goods and services."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If MORTGAGE30US drops >0.5% in 2 weeks AND Google Trends 'refinance' searches rise >40% YoY, buy XLY",
      "exit": "After 12 trading days or when rates stabilize for 3+ consecutive days",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Fed policy shifts and bond market repricing trigger rate drops several times per year, reliably firing this signal."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Copper Contango Collapse Industrial Demand Proxy",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series MMNRNJ (Copper prices, spot) daily for spot; pair with CME futures curve slope via price_only adapter (HG nearest vs 6-month forward prices)",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When copper spot rallies but forward curve flattens (contango narrows), market is signaling near-term demand strength but supply confidence; bullish for materials.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLB",
      "rationale": "Copper demand elasticity is high for construction, manufacturing, and power; curve shape is a sensitive real-economy gauge."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If copper spot rises >2% in 3 days AND 6-month forward premium (contango) shrinks to <1.5% annualized",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or if contango widens beyond 2.5%",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Commodity curve dynamics shift weekly with supply/demand news; this signal fires 2–3 times per month."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Healthcare Procedure Google Trends Demand Resurgence",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search volume for 'cosmetic surgery near me', 'dental implant cost', 'laser eye surgery'",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in elective procedure searches signal consumer confidence and disposable income recovery; bullish for consumer discretionary and healthcare services.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Elective healthcare spending is a proxy for consumer financial health and willingness to spend on non-essentials."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly Google Trends for elective procedure searches rises >50% YoY and XLY closes up >0.5% that week",
      "exit": "After 3 weeks or if search interest falls below baseline for 2 consecutive weeks",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 18,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal consumer confidence waves (post-bonus, tax refunds, holiday spending) reliably drive elective procedure searches."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Airline Load Factor Contraction Fare Collapse Signal",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) weekly airline load factor data via bts_airline_load_factor adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When airline load factors drop >5% week-over-week, it signals weakening travel demand and airlines will cut fares aggressively, pressuring XLY and supporting XLP.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Falling load factors and fare wars erode airline and leisure travel margins; consumer is trading down or reducing discretionary trips."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly load factor falls >5% WoW and is below 12-week MA",
      "exit": "After 4 weeks or when load factor recovers above 12-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal travel patterns and economic news cause load factor swings monthly; signal fires 2–4 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Agricultural Commodity Volatility Inversion Farmland Stress",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series PPIACO (Corn price) and PPIWHE (Wheat price) daily; calculate 10-day rolling volatility for each",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When corn and wheat volatility invert (one spikes while the other collapses), it signals supply/demand dislocations that stress rural credit and farm equipment demand.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Agricultural equipment manufacturers (John Deere, AGCO) are sensitive to farm profitability; commodity volatility inversions signal margin compression."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 10-day corn vol rises >50% while wheat vol falls >30%, and XLI closes down >1%",
      "exit": "After 6 trading days or when both volatilities normalize within 20% of each other",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal weather, USDA reports, and geopolitical events drive commodity vol spikes monthly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Bankruptcy Filings Surge Corporate Stress Wave",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "US Courts public bankruptcy filing data (Chapter 7, 11, 13) via html_table scrape; weekly aggregation of new filings",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in corporate bankruptcy filings (Chapter 11) signal debt stress across the economy; lag 1–2 weeks before credit spreads widen and equities selloff.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Rising corporate bankruptcies increase bank loan losses and reduce confidence in credit quality; financial sector reprices risk higher."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly Chapter 11 filings exceed 12-week MA by >20% and XLF closes down >0.5%",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when weekly filings fall below 12-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Economic cycles and credit tightening events drive bankruptcy waves; signal fires 2–3 times per year."
  }
]
```
